claim: A resolver signoff on a DECIDED/HELD Conflict or a transitioned Assumption shall be preserved as a Signoff payload IN the graph node (not only in gitignored proposal JSON) -- decided_by, date, verbatim (optional), instrument and chosen_variant (for HELD->DECIDED) are auditable from the substrate.
why: K2(a) from lens-1-ontology review: AssumptionTransition's decided_by was required at validation but the writer wrote only status+reason to the graph, so the signature evaporated into gitignored JSON -- R-trust-anchor-mechanism was not auditable from the substrate. K2(b): HELD->DECIDED erased Variants (the non-chosen implies/costs -- anti-relitigation cargo -- were overwritten); the chosen variant lived only as prose inside DECIDED(...). The Signoff payload is ontology.Signoff (internal/ontology/signoff.go): a struct (NOT a new node type, anti-RDF -- mirrors Variant's rationale) carrying DecidedBy + Date + Verbatim + Instrument + ChosenVariant, attached as *Signoff on Conflict.Signoff and Assumption.Signoff (internal/ontology/conflict.go, internal/ontology/assumption.go). The proposal writer builds it: ProposedConflictTransition.mutate (internal/proposal/mutate.go) sets c.Signoff = &ontology.Signoff{...} when the transition is a decision with a non-empty DecidedBy (Instrument defaults to SignoffInstrumentPersonal), and ProposedAssumptionTransition.mutate does the equivalent for assumptions; variants are preserved across HELD->DECIDED. Instrument (SignoffInstrumentPersonal / SignoffInstrumentDEL, internal/ontology/signoff.go) is an explicit seam for the future verifiable-signature wave (R-decided-by-verifiable-signature is OPEN -- this atom does NOT bind to git/crypto, only names the seam). check_signoff_chosen_variant_resolves (internal/invariants/typed_anchors.go) enforces a non-empty chosen_variant is the id of one of the conflict's variants; check_decided_conflict_carries_signoff enforces consistency (signoff.decided_by == conflict.decided_by) when a signoff is present, SOFT (pre-existing decisions without signoff are legitimate, no migration forcing).
